body:
closes #114  - This PR adds `Var.apply_landmask` and `Var.apply_oceanmask` which sets all the coordinates corresponding to land or ocean to zeros respectively. Every function that compute bias or RMSE now has a mask parameter which take in either mask functions to correctly compute the bias or RMSE.

The plotting functions now use the appropriate mask which solve the problem of masking not affecting the colorbar.

A bias plot with a land mask looks like this:
![plot_bias_landmask](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee397327-621c-4f12-a1b2-40c591c1adc5)

A bias plot with an ocean mask looks like this:
![plot_bias_oceanmask](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/91e0dc75-3909-4ddd-8e1e-bc3b08992ca9)

Plotting the land mask gives this:
![plot_apply_land_mask](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b0c7afd8-b6d2-425c-ba41-71013e687e2d)

Plotting the ocean mask gives this:
![plot_apply_ocean_mask](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/691deff1-e7f8-4f69-8d57-4c11fc60eae8)
title: Add masks for land and ocean

body:
closes #116  - Before, the function Var.shift_to_start_of_previous_month does not has error handling for duplicate dates.
 This PR adds a check for duplicate dates and throws an error if there are duplicate dates.
title: Add error handling for duplicate dates

body: closes #112 - For the functions `_geomakie_plot_on_globe!`, `heatmap2D!`, and `line_plot1D!`, you cannot initialize multiple figures and use these functions afterward as you get the error "LoadError: There is no current axis to plot into". To fix this, we initialize an `Axis` object in the function and use that for plotting.
title: Fix bug with plotting multiple figures
